entry.title: ASR Integration With SAP EWM - Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
Configuration

Make the Shipping Point Relevant for Advanced Shipping and Receiving

Tcode- /N/SCMTMS/LOC3

 Define Freight Unit Types

 

Freight Unit Building Rule- FUBR- Fiori App

Define Logistics Integration Profile

Define Freight Order Types

Activate BC Sets- Inbound and Outbound

I will add both Inbound and Outbound BC Sets which needs to be activated.

Inbound Delivery BC Set

Activate BC Set /SCWM/DLV_INBOUND_FO for EWM document type IFO

/SCWM/DLV_INBOUND_FO

Enable the item type IDFO for the use of serial numbers

In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Delivery – Warehouse Request → Quantity Offsetting → Define Quantity Offsetting Profiles

               Select the profile „/SCWM/INB_PRD_DLV_FO” and choose the view “Assignment of Quantity Roles” on the left side.

               For the quantity role “SN”, deselect the „Inactive“ checkbox.

 

Map Document Type from ERP System to EWM Document Type IFO

               DocTypeERP   EL

               Doc. Type         IFO

Map Item Type from ERP System to EWM Item Type IDFO

               DocTypeERP   EL

               ItmTpERP          ELN

               Doc. Type         IFO

               Item Type         IDFO

Now you need to do determination of WPT according to your Doc type and Item type

 

Activate BC Set /SCWM/DLV_INBOUND_PACK

If you want to automatically create packaging items for the packaging materials of HUs in the EWM inbound delivery, you need to activate this BC set. This BC set includes the EWM item type IPHU.

 

Automatically Generate Packaging Items for EWM Inbound Delivery

In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Goods Receipt Process → Inbound Delivery → Automatically Generate Packaging Items for Inbound Deliveries.

               Warehouse Number                 ZS01   

               Doc. Cat.                                        PDI

               Doc. Type                                       IFO

               Dlv. Item creation                       X

               Implicit GR                                    X

               Locking During Creation                        Blank

Open the view “Relevance of Packmaterials” and select the packaging type for the delivery item creation, for example, packing material type ‘PALT’.

Open the view „Determination of Delivery Item Type“, and enter the following data:

               Packaging Material Type         PALT

               Warehouse Number                 ZS01

               Doc. Cat.                                        PDI

               Doc. Type                                       IFO

               Item Type                                       IPHU

               Stock Type                                     F1

Define Delivery Item Category Determination for the Usage of Automatically Generated Packaging Items in EWM Inbound Delivery

In Customizing, choose Logistics Execution → Shipping → Deliveries → Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries.

               Delivery Type                                EL

               Item Category Group                VERP

               Usage                                              Blank

               ItemCat - HighLevItm                             Blank

               Default Item Category                            ELP

Add Transaction Code to Movement Type for the Usage of Automatically Generated Packaging Item in EWM Inbound Delivery

If you want to automatically create packaging items for the packaging materials of HUs in the EWM inbound delivery, you need to perform the following Customizing activity. This setting is necessary for the goods receipt reversal process

  1. In Customizing, choose Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Movement Types → Copy, Change Movement types.
  2. Select the „Movement type“ checkbox.
  3. Enter the selection range from movement type 502 to movement type 502.
  4. Select the view “Allowed Transactions” and add the transaction code VL09.

Outbound Delivery BC Set

Activate BC Set /SCWM/DLV_OUTBOUND_FO for EWM Document Type OFO

  1. From the initial screen, enter transaction SCPR20 or /NSCPR20.
  2. Enter /SCWM/DLV_OUTBOUND_FO in the „BC SET“ field and choose “Activate BC Set” (F7). Check the BCS documentation and adapt the settings for your process. Enable the Item Type ODFO for the Use of Serial Numbers

If you use materials with a serial number requirement in EWM in the outbound process, you need to enable the item type ODFO for the use of serial numbers.

  1. In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Delivery – Warehouse Request → Quantity Offsetting → Define Quantity Offsetting Profiles.
  2. Select the profile /SCWM/OUT_PRD_DLV_FO and choose the view „Assignment of Quantity Roles“ on the left side.
  3. For the quantity role “SN”, deselect the “Inactive” checkbox. Define Process Profile for Document Header

You can enable the immediate quantity update to the LE delivery for quantity changes in the outbound delivery order, and immediate handling unit update to the LE delivery with the creation of the EWM outbound delivery.

In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Delivery – Warehouse Request → Process Management and Control → Define Process Profile for Document Header.

Enable immediate quantity update

  1. Select profile /SCWM/OUT_PRD_FO, and choose “‘Details”.
  2. Select the checkbox for ‘Quantity Update’.

Enable immediate handling unit update

  1. Select profile /SCWM/OUT_FD_FO and choose “Details”.
  2. Select the checkbox for ‘HU Update’.

Map Document Type from ERP System to EWM Document Type OFO

  1. In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM.
  2. Map ERP document type LF to EWM document type OFO.

               DocTypeERP                  LF

               Doc. Type                        OFO

Map Item Type from ERP System to EWM Item Type ODFO

  1. In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP System to EWM.
  2. Map ERP document type LF, ERP item type TAN, and EWM document type OFO to EWM item type ODFO.

               DocTypeERP   LF

               ItmTpERP          TAN

               Doc. Type         OFO

               Item Type         ODFO

Determine the WPY based on your Doc Type

Maintain POD Relevance

  1. In Customizing, choose Logistics Execution → Shipping → Deliveries → Proof of Delivery → Set POD-Relevance Depending on Delivery Item Category.

               Item Category               TAN

               POD-relevant V (Copy POD relevance from preceding document)

               POD automatic            blank

Create Location – Loading/Unloading Point

Staging Area and Door Determination (Outbound)

/SCWM/STADET_OUT - Staging Area and Door Determination (Outbound)

Assignment of Unloading Points to Warehouse Doors

/n/scwm/door_scu

I have Completed the Configuration, Now Running and understanding the process is very much important.

Sine There will not be having any TU available the reversal process would be easy for the warehouse side, I will probably cover this topic in my upcoming blogs.

Testing/Running The Process

Create Sales order- It will create Freight Unit automatically.

 

 

Create Fright Ordre – based on Planning

 

Freight Order will be created based on your Planning Profile and Your Resource Master, Lane master, and Zone Master.

 

Since I am not using Tendering Process, I will assign the carrier Manually.

 

Now you create the Delivery from SAP TM- (0052 Control Key is required to create OD from SO)

As you can see from the screenshot below, "Send Loading Instruction" is disabled because the integration is ASR-based, and ASR does not support TU. For TU-based integration, please refer to my previous blog post. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-members/crucial-key-setup-needed-for-the-integration-of-tm-tu-based-integration/ba-p/13766871

 

Based on your SLoc in SO, the created Delivery will be distributed to EWM. I would like you to notice the difference between the OD and warehouse OD (if your EWM no range for OD differ, you can see that both nodes differ; this is the beauty of ASR, having both delivery with its differentiation).

Check the delivery in SAP EWM with Transportation Planning Type – F (EWM Level) and TM Controlled one will be E.

Actually, ASR support 2 levels, EWM controlled one and TM controlled one (the TM controlled one you can see only in S4 2023, I will add the path in the last fold of this blog)

Basically EWM controlled meaning, the OBD will not require any events from TM to start the warehouse process, where as TM controlled one require TM system to send you "Set in-Execution" until that time the OD will be in Block Status.

 

SAP Has given Specific App for ASR- Load or Unload Freight Orders, Here you can enter your Freight Order no and Warehouse no, and you can monitor your complete Warehouse activities.

 

 

Now you can process and complete the warehousing process (Picking, Packing but not Loading), you can go to the warehouse monitor and You need to do the ready for Shipping.

 By this time your Warehouse processing status will be- Completed. (Goto Status tab in FO).

You need to process the status - Set Ready for Transportation Execution

The Status will be in-Execution

Since the Transpiration Execution has been started, you need to set the process of FO In-Execution.

The Status will be changed to In-Execution.

Since the Truck Physically Arrived to your Warehouse for Loading, you need to set your FO for check-in, and for all these steps, relevant executions/events are getting triggered in background.

 The Evens will be below.

Your warehouse can do the loading by RF(Upon loading completion the OBD header status will be changed to Loading completion, and same you can see in the EWM OBD, as well as Load Unload Freight Order App).

Event

Now From The Load Unload Freight Order App, you should make it as loading completion.

The Loading Status will be change to Green(Completed)

Now you can post the GI from The Load Unload Freight Order Fiori App

Now all the process will be completed till GI.

 

There is some important point I would like to Add here.

1. If the events are not getting triggered, you need to raise it to SAP, there will be some notes which SAP will suggest to implement.

2. For TM Controlled ASR, in the SAP TM- Integration- You will find new Node there, where you can controlled the TM based ASR, and there are limitations are there, you need to read the Product guideline for that(Its only available from S4 2023).

In addition, I would like to thank my Mentor Mr. Ramakrishna Pantula for his guidance and My friend Mr. Tabrez Shaikh for this thorough ASR implementation.

Thanks & Regards,

Muhammad Ashraf

entry.title: Integration between SAP Yard Logistics and Dock Appointment Scheduling
entry.content:
I want to go over the operation of SAP Yard Logistics and Dock Appointment Scheduling in this blog post. To ensure that the warehouse runs efficiently and that the workload is distributed throughout the working day, it is crucial to schedule vehicle arrivals as well as the loading and unloading of the trucks. The carriers can enrol their transportation units for the yard and schedule time slots for un- and uploading with SAP Dock Appointment Scheduling, which is used to efficiently plan vehicle arrival in your warehouses. The planning document, or yard request (YR), is where the Yard Logistics process begins. I have manually prepared a yard request that is pertinent to the dock appointment here.

Configuration

 

Maintain Loading Appointment Relevance for Yard Order Type

 

Maintain Loading Appointment Relevance for Yard Order Type and TU Type

Control Automatic Yard Request Release

 

 Assign Document Categories for Dock Appointment Scheduling

Define Number Range Intervals for Appointment Numbers

Create Docking Location – Fiori App

 

 

Generating the time slot and establishing the warehouse door capacity at a given moment is the most crucial step.

When selecting a docking site, make sure to complete this setup; otherwise, you won't receive the appointment. Scroll down and select "Create Timeslots" in the same app.

The system will launch a new popup window where you'll need to specify when actual loading will take place in the warehouse, which is the warehouse that occasionally operates at capacity (the number of doors in your warehouse).

The break timing can be set to 0, which means that no slots will be available at that time.

Create Loading Point – NWBC

You need to assign the Docking Location to Loading Point, I have not integrated with EWM, that is the reason I have maintained :SAP EWM Integration- No Integration" I will cover in my next blog how to integrated YL EWM TM.

Assign Docking Location and Loading Point to Yard Number (/SAPYL/VASDKL) – NWBC- Settings

Maintain Door Assignment to Docking Location and Loading Point (/SAPYL/VDSDORST)

Test Cycle

Create Yard Request-

You can see in the below screenshot, upon creation of Yard request System triggered the DAS Appointment.

Now Go to Dock Appointment Scheduling Tab, and you can see your DAS appointment no, click on Go to Appointment Button, system will popup new window.

Here you can see your Appointment capacity, available timings.

Select any time.

Save, and Go to Yard Request, DAS Tab, your appointment is updated there.

As we have configured the system to generate an automated yard order, it will do so upon creation of YR.

Your Yard Order will be in Planned Status and Consistent.

System will create Yard Task Automatically based on your config.

And Same DAS Appointment will flow to your YO.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Muhammad Ashraf

entry.title: SAP Yard Logistics - Chapter 1
entry.content:
This blog post will discuss how to implement SAP Yard Logistics in your company. Since you already have SAP YL, which is an add-on that you can purchase separately from SAP, please remember that SAP YL is always an add-on for your Embedded S4 HANA system. It is important to note that SAP Yard Logistics is designed to optimize yard operations, such as managing trailers and containers, improving efficiency, and reducing costs. By integrating SAP YL with your existing SAP system, you can streamline processes and enhance visibility across your supply chain.  

Many features set SAP YL apart from SAP EWM Yard Management. With SAP EWM Yard Management, you can only manage your warehouse at a very basic level. In contrast, with SAP YL, you can plan your schedules, loading and unloading procedures, and yard operations with feature-rich features like real-time process optimization and monitoring (e.g Cleaning process, TU Quality check, Movements, loading/unloading and so on).

The process will become fantastic for your business when your YL meets with DAS, EWM, and TM.

I wanted to specifically inform you all that your YL can be linked to EWM TM and PM.

Remember that your SAP YL warehouse will utilize the foundation of EWM and operate on top of its core functionality when you begin implementing SAP YL. This will help you avoid confusion with EWM warehouse.

Since you are likely implementing SAP YL in your system for the first time, please confirm that you have completed the setup listed below in order for SAP YL to function.

Let's find out the secret. I will discuss SAP YL configuration and process chapter by chapter in my next blogs.

Configuration

 

Maintain Text Schema

One of the manuals SAP YL provides for preserving and sharing texts from older documents is text schemas.

 

Maintain Attachment Type Schema

SAP YL We deal with attachments related to business documents on a daily basis. You have the ability to store, view, download, and print attachment files.

Define Warehouse Numbers

The warehouse for SAP YL is different from the SAP EWM warehouse; if you are integrating SAP YL with SAP EWM, you should use a different name in the SAP YL warehouse; otherwise, you will not be able to connect your EWM warehouse and YL warehouse. As I previously stated, don't get confused with warehouse creation if you are from EWM.

Define Warehouse Number Control

These are the essential details that you must provide in order for the warehouse to function (weights, measurements, time, volume, etc).

 

 

 

Create Supply chain unit-  /N/SCMB/SCUMAIN

Assign Warehouse Numbers

Define Yard Numbers - SAP YL WH

Define Number Ranges for Yard Request, Yard Order, Yard Task

Maintain Below-

Define Number Ranges for Yard Request

Define Number Ranges for Yard Order

Define Number Ranges for Yard Task

Define Checkpoints

Define Transportation Unit Types

Define Number Range Interval for Transportation Units

Define Number Ranges for S&R Activities

Maintain Means of Transport

Define Control Parameters for Forming Vehicles/Transportation Units

Define Order Types

Assign Order Type and Threshold for Arrival

Define Date and Time Record Types

 

 

 

Define Warehouse Process Type

Define Activity Types

Define Default Activity Types

 

 

 

 

Define Storage Type

Define Storage Section

Define Storage Bin Types

Load Storage Bin

Goto NEBC à Master Data

Assign Checkpoint to Yard Bin and SCU (/SCWM/YM_CHKPT_BIN)

NWBC – Master Data

Link - Packaging Mat. (TU) and MTr (/SAPYL/PM_MTR)

NWBC – Master Data

Define Default Location for Yard Order/Yard Request (/SAPYL/DEF_LOCAT)

Define Number Ranges for Yard Request, Yard Order, Yard Task

Basic Test –

 

 

 

 

Save It, System will create Yard Request with Status as – Created

You need to Release the Yard Request to Create Yard Order Automatically.

 

System has created Yard Order Automatically and All the Task which was assigned with Yard Order in Configuration are available in the Yard Order.

Thanks For Reading my blogs.

Thanks & Regards,

Muhammad Ashraf

entry.title: SAP Yard Logistics - Chapter 2
entry.content:
This blog post is being written for the SAP Yard Logistics Outbound Process. In this post, we will explore the key features and benefits of the SAP Yard Logistics Outbound Process, as well as provide tips for successful implementation. Additionally, we will discuss common challenges faced by organizations when utilizing this process and offer solutions to overcome them.

Configuration

Define Order Types

Assign Order Type and Threshold for Arrival

Define Date and Time Record Types

Define Default Activity Types

Yard Logistics - Yard Tasks 

 

Define Number Ranges for Yard Request, Yard Order, Yard Task

Yard Logistics - Master Data - Setting Up the Yard – Assign Number Range Intervals to Yard Request

Yard Logistics - Master Data - Setting Up the Yard – Assign Number Range Intervals to Yard Order

Test Cycle

Create Yard Request

Click on Release

You can see the Yard task are linked to your yard order

 

You must check in for the TU. In order to check in for the outbound scenario, you must activate the outbound yard order. You will notice that there is a button at the top to switch to the inbound yard order; in my scenario, we wanted the inbound yard order to be created automatically to give the operator flexibility in automation.

To activate the yard order, please follow the below steps.

 

Yard Order will be activated

 

Now Switch to Inbound yard order and perform the check in.

 

 

The inbound yard order will be completed upon check in.

 

A concept pertaining to scenario and document creation level is something I would like to share with you.

Let's assume that EWM TM and YL are integrated (I have worked on this integration level; however, because the test system isn't configured correctly to perform the necessary tests, I have to create them by hand).

The EWM system will have the TU created and in the Planned status once the TM system sends the loading instruction. In a similar vein, the Yard Request will also be created automatically.

Outbound Scenario- Yard request will be generated. (Once Yard Request is released -- Outbound Yard Order will be created (the yard task will be linked in accordance with your configuration) ---- An inbound yard order will be generated either automatically or manually (depending on your configuration)--- the significance of Inbound yard order will only ever be used for check-in purposes. (Every task which is your activity will be carried out according to your execution document under the Yard Order).

Inbound Scenario- Yard request will be generated. (Once Yard Request is released -- Inbound Yard Order will be created (the yard task will be linked in accordance with your configuration) ---- An Outbound yard order will be generated either automatically or manually (depending on your configuration)--- the significance of Outbound yard order will only ever be used for check-out purposes. (Every task which is your activity will be carried out according to your execution document under the Yard Order).

We need to confirm the task, which is simply the execution document that maps your actual ongoing on-the-ground activity.

Lets do the Action!!!!!!!!!

As previously stated in my blog, activities involving movement will require the destination bin to activate the task; however, activities that do not involve movement will not require the destination bin to activate.

There are two methods to turn it on: automatically (I'll go over all the automation in my next blog post) or manually by hitting the "start processing" button.

 

 

Select the task number. (In my upcoming blog article, I'll discuss resource and queue assignment as well as YouTube confirmation based on mobile app.)

Select "Start."

The Status will become In-Process

Click on Confirm

 

Loading Unloading Task is confirmed

 

Assign Bin Manually, by hitting edit Button.

 

Perform the same activity to confirm the yard task

Assign the checkout area

 

Perform the same activity to confirm the yard task

Assign the checkout area

Follow the same procedure. Once the garden work is finished and the checkout is complete, your EWM will reflect this. However, certain activities that are connected to your EWM require a separate setup in order to transfer data to it.

Finally, finish the checkout so that the system recognizes that the TU has left your yard.

Status of your YO

Status of your Yard Request Doc Flow.

 

Please read my Chapter 1 Blog.

Thanks & Regards

Muhammad Ashraf

entry.title: Restricting EWM Inspection lot generation - The usage of Badi /SCWM/EX_QM_INSP_RELEVANCE
entry.content:
Requirement: System should not generate EWM Quality inspection lot when Vendor is sending same Supplier /SAP batch. If the supplier or SAP batch has ever been inspected, EWM should skip generating inspection lot and post GR into unrestricted stock type.

Problem Addressed/ Standard Scenario: There is no SAP standard functionality which allows to skip inspection lot creation based on above scenario when its product having Supplier/ SAP batch already undergone Usage decision. Standard SAP keeps generating inspection lot or add into common inspection lot using ' Inspection Lot Summary. But there is no workaround in standard to avoid generating 'Inspection Lot' if SAP/Supplier batch is already inspected.

Process: During receiving against Purchase Order either from Fiori or RFUI, system creates inspection lot for inspection type '01' for corresponding Supplier/SAP batch. Latter, result recording and Usage decision is conducted for the required supplier/ SAP batch with acceptance or rejection. 

Solution: Since there is no standard functionality available to address the above requirement, SAP has provided Badi /SCWM/EX_QM_INSP_RELEVANCE  which can be used to prevent inspection lot creation according to the business requirement.( e.g. for a Product and its batch is already inspected) .This BAdI is available for both decentralized and embedded landscape.

During first time SAP batch receiving 0000000140, system creating corresponding QI inspection with Q4 stock type. 

In same vendor lot/ SAP batch of the required product is received again. standard SAP would either create a new inspection lot or with inspection lot summary it will club this batch into already created inspection lot( Not inspected) using 'Inspection Lot Summary' as below

 

 Badi Implementation: Using SAP Badi /SCWM/EX_QM_INSP_RELEVANCE, it is possible to add a logic of stopping generation of inspection lot if Supplier/SAP batch has already been inspection. 

We build a logic reading QALS table if Product/Batch is already inspected in any of the plants, avoid creation of inspection lot generation using changing parameter of Badi.

In IS_QINSP_DATA importing parameter in Badi, customer can read many fields & can build their logic on custom requirement( E.g Avoid creation of inspection of certain document type or item type ) using CV_NOT_RELEVANT as influencing parameter.

Post Badi Implementation: Post Badi implementation, system allowed to skip inspection lot creation for Same Vendor/SAP batch combination in below example.

In below example, a shipment is received already received and inspected for Supplier Batch VEND006/ SAP batch 0000000140.

UD is completed.

In QALS table, inspection is marked with UD.

Receiving the same Vendor/ SAP batch combination, badi allowed to skip inspection lot creation.

On top of that, customer has to implement SAP note:3200795 - Stock type remains in quality when inspection is set to not relevant via BAdI. Even if you implement the badi, system would skip the inspection lot creation, but the stock of inbound delivery items remain in  'Q4' stock type. The above note fixes the issue of stock type change from Q4 to F2 at GR or at Warehouse task creation

Thanks for reading. All test is performed in S/4 HANA 2022 Sandbox Embedded EWM system

The blog is Co-Authored by  @prem_swaroop   my colleague from SAP labs India.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits Series -EWM Data Management in a Nutshell
entry.content:
During the implementation phase of an EWM project, many customers and partners tend to be heavily focused on the implementation itself, without paying sufficient attention to data management after go-live. This can have a significant impact on the system's long-term performance and may even lead to difficulties in meeting legal requirements.

Throughout the day-to-day warehouse business transactions, SAP tables are filled with various types of data, such as deliveries, warehouse tasks, warehouse orders, logs, change documents, and PPF actions.

It is crucial to define a comprehensive methodology to determine:

  1. What data should be avoided from being stored in the system? 
  2. What data should be regularly deleted ?
  3. What data should be archived for long-term storage and compliance purposes?

lets have quick look into the objective of Data Management:

  • Greater system availability through reduced data volumes in the database
  • Better use of resources utilization.
  • Improve system performance
  • Support in complying with legal data retention requirements through more automatization and more complete data archiving and storage processes

Before Talking about what are SAP Methodology for Data Management in EWM, its essential to understand Management strategy and their KPIs toward Managing the data and retaining these data on the system, Retention time can be maintained in Customizing, That's a prerequisites 

Data Management in EWM consisted of the following :

1  Avoidances :

This refers to the practice of avoiding the storage of data that is not important for the business to retain in the live EWM system. and the goal is to prevent unnecessary data growth a

2 Deletion :

From short-term perspective the data can be kept on the system in number of days for analyses and get deleted afterwards.

For Deletion & Avoidance Methodologies, in Operations Guide for SAP S/4HANA 2023 section 10.6.2 Extended Warehouse Management, describes all tasks that can be automated and recommended Frequency. It is important that you monitor the successful execution of these tasks on a regular basis. 

Example 

Operations Guide for SAP S/4HANA 2023

Note :

Objects like Application Log, Change Documents, PPF and qRFC-trace/log Information, can be deleted and avoided, the decision on which Methodology to be chosen its back to management strategy and visions

3- Archiving 

Archiving refers to the process associated with copying data and supporting documents from an active system (SAP) to an external source which no longer required in the system from the database, and it can be retrieve via archive file access. 

However there are prerequisites has to meet to be able to archive the object as following in addition to that there are some dependencies between EWM archiving objects which need to be considered for example Vehicle activities WME_VEH can be only archived if they are completed and the related TU activities WME_TU are already archived.

To find out more about what are depended please check this note 1830178 - Archiving dependencies between EWM archiving objects

 

Let us have Delivery archiving as example :

Prerequisites :

  • Completion (DCO) status is completed (9).
  • Successor documents (FDO) are preprocessed Report: /SCWM/POST_PROC_DLV_SET_DOY.
  • Archiving (DAC) status has value Yes (X) Report: /SCDL/BO_ARCHIVE.
  • Assigned HUs are archived Archiving Object: WME_HU.
  • Assigned TUs are completed (CHKO): Transaction: /SCWM/TU or /SCWM/CICO.
  • Assigned VAS orders are archived: Archiving Object: WME_VAS.
  • ECC-messages and LIME-entries are completely processed.

Write :   /SCDL/DLV_INB_ARCH_WRITE

The write program reads the data records of the documents to be archived from the database and records them in an archive file

Delete:  /SCDL/DLV_INB_ARCH_DELETE

The delete program deletes the data records from the database that were previously archived with the write program.

Read: /SCDL/DLV_INB_ACRH_READ

The read program reads the archived data records. In the selection screen of the read program, you can select the documents to be archived via the selection criteria in Deliveries and Restrictions

To find more about write, delete, read and read Archiving procedures for all other objects please visit our SAP Help Data Archiving in Extended Warehouse Management | SAP Help Portal

Most important notes :

2388483 – How-To: Data Management for Technical Tables

1065952 - Deactivating the PPF log (performance in EWM)

1975173 - Delete unnecessary PPF triggers

1567007 - Deleting entries from the QRFCTRACE table

1423066 - Optimization of the performance in EWM

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement Service can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your project a success!

If you would like to learn more on how Premium Engagement Services can support you during your transformation of EWM and TM to SAP S/4HANA, have a look at SAP Blog 

Transformation to SAP S/4HANA for Warehouse and Transportation Management

entry.title: Importance of receiving staging area and STADET_IN transaction in EWM
entry.content:
Introduction:

In the realm of warehouse management, staging areas play a pivotal role in optimizing the receipt and processing of goods. Effective staging areas ensure that products are managed efficiently, reducing errors, and improving overall operational efficiency. In this blog, I will explore the importance of staging areas within a warehouse and how SAP EWM as a WMS can significantly enhance control over inbound deliveries and guide goods to their designated receiving areas.

The Importance of Different Staging Areas in Warehouse Layouts

In designing an efficient warehouse layout, incorporating various staging areas is key for optimizing operations and ensuring a smooth workflow. Each staging area is tailored to handle specific types of inventory and processing needs.

Bulk Items Staging Area

For bulk items, warehouses often include a staging area with larger doors and ample space. This dedicated area facilitates the efficient receipt and storage of large quantities of goods. By having a separate space for bulk items, warehouses can streamline the unloading process and manage these items more effectively, ensuring that they are stored and handled appropriately.

Perishables and Hazardous Materials

Perishables and hazardous materials require specialized handling due to their unique characteristics. Separate receiving areas for these types of goods ensure they are stored under optimal conditions and comply with safety regulations. For perishables, this might include refrigeration or controlled environments, while hazardous materials need specific safety protocols and containment measures to prevent accidents and ensure regulatory compliance.

Intensive Quality Inspection and Counting

Some products necessitate detailed quality inspections and accurate counting upon arrival. Dedicated receiving areas for these items allow for focused attention and thorough examination. This separation helps ensure that products meet quality standards and are correctly accounted for before they move further into the warehouse or distribution process.

High-Value Items

Tracking and managing high-value items often require a higher level of security and precision. By allocating separate receiving areas for these goods, warehouses can enhance inventory control, reduce the risk of loss or theft, and ensure that these valuable items are handled with the necessary care.

Safety and Regulatory Compliance

Certain products, such as chemicals and pharmaceuticals, come with stringent safety regulations. Having designated receiving areas for these items ensures adherence to safety standards and regulations. This separation helps in managing the special handling requirements and minimizes the risk of contamination or accidents, ensuring a safe working environment.

By integrating different staging areas into warehouse layouts, businesses can improve operational efficiency, enhance safety, and ensure effective inventory management. Each area is designed to handle specific types of goods, facilitating a more organized and streamlined warehouse operation.

How STADET_IN helps

In the inbound delivery process, determining the goods movement bin (GMBIN) is crucial for smooth and error-free inbound delivery. The system identifies the GM bin based on the warehouse process type (source storage type and source bin maintained in WPT settings)

However, if there is a business need to receive products from different suppliers in distinct areas, even if the WPT remains the same, the system should accommodate these requirements. For example:

Product A from Supplier X should be received in GR AREA 1, which is set up for mixed product reception and requires deconsolidation.

Product A from Supplier Y should be received in GR AREA 2, which is designated for fully packed pallets and does not require deconsolidation.

To propose the correct staging area, SAP EWM offers a functionality(transaction) called /SCWM/STADET_IN. This transaction allows you to maintain the necessary details, enabling the system to suggest the appropriate staging area.

Based on the supplier (ship-from), we can assign different staging areas and doors for receiving, even when the same WPT is in use.

However, don’t forget to maintain the access sequence for this setup. Use transaction /SCWM/STADET_ASS to configure it accordingly.

We will test this in the system using two different inbound deliveries for the same material, each from a different supplier.

Inbound delivery- 180000087

Inbound delivery- 180000088

Now, let’s explore another example of how to determine the staging area using the staging area/door determination group, which is typically maintained in each product master. For instance, heavy products might require different staging doors (larger doors), while lightweight materials can be accommodated through standard doors and receiving areas.

First, maintain the staging area/door determination group in the product master,

/SCWM/MAT1

We can configure this value in the SPRO,

Now maintain the staging area/door determination group in the /SCWM/STADET_IN

First, assign the appropriate staging area/door determination group to the product master. Then, maintain this group in the STADET_IN transaction.

Material

Description

Staging area/door determination group

TG101

Bulk trading goods

Z003

TG102

Lightweight trading goods

Z001

Also, maintain the /SCWM/STADET_ASS

Check the below screenshot,

Please note that all testing and screenshots are taken from the S4HANA 2023 demo system. This demo environment is used to simulate real-world scenarios and validate configurations. As such, the screenshots and results presented are based on this specific system version and may differ from other environments or versions.

entry.title: Single order staging process flow
entry.content:
There are two types of staging processes namely:

1)      Single order staging

2)      Cross order staging

 

1) Single Order Staging:

Single Order Staging is a logistics and supply chain management concept where the process of preparing and staging inventory for a single order is executed. This process involves gathering, organizing, and preparing the items specified in a single customer order before they are dispatched for delivery. Single Order Staging is particularly important for order fulfillment and ensures that each order is processed accurately and efficiently. In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), single-order staging allows users to request that components be staged for a specific order. This involves moving components from a warehouse to a production supply area (PSA).

 

2) Cross Order Staging:

Cross Order Staging is a logistics strategy used to optimize the handling and preparation of multiple orders simultaneously. Unlike Single Order Staging, which focuses on preparing one order at a time, Cross Order Staging involves organizing and staging items from multiple orders together in a way that can improve efficiency and reduce overall handling time. For cross-order staging, the system proposes a quantity for staging for each component that is required on the PSA. The system considers only PMRs with the status Released. You can create warehouse tasks for the replenishment with a different quantity to the quantity proposed by the system.

 

Process Order:

A Process Order is a term commonly used in manufacturing and production environments, particularly in industries that use process-based production methods, such as chemical, pharmaceutical, or food and beverage industries. It refers to a detailed plan or instruction set for producing a specific quantity of a product, typically involving multiple steps or stages in the production process.

 

PMR:

In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM), a production material request (PMR) is a document that contains information about the materials required for a manufacturing order. This information includes the products, quantities, production supply areas, and dates for which the materials will be staged. The PMR is created in SAP EWM after a manufacturing order is created and released in the ERP system. The PMR acts as a link between production planning and extended warehouse management, and is essential for efficient production.

 

Staging:

Staging refers to the process of preparing and organizing goods or materials in a designated area or staging location within a warehouse before they are either shipped out to customers, used in production, or moved to another location within the warehouse. Staging is a crucial step in warehouse management and inventory handling as it helps to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and ensure that goods are ready for the next phase in the supply chain.

 

Single Order Staging Process Flow:

Process Order:

Create the Process Order using the transaction code COR1

Provide the below details and save the Process order:

a)      Materials

b)      Basic dates (start and end)

 

 

PMR:

Navigate to T-code /N/SCWM/PMR to open Maintain Prd Material Request.

Click the drop down in Find and select Manufacturing order.

Enter Process Order.

Click the Execute button.

PMR Document will be displayed.

Verify PMR for Process Orders- Item and header details for correctness like staging method, product, qty, bin, PSA, Process type for Single order staging

 

Staging:

Navigate to /N/SCWM/STAGE to schedule staging for production

Provide the below details:

1)      Warehouse number

2)      Staging method

3)      Manufacturing Order

De-select Test mode checkbox and then click on the execute button.

 

Five warehouse tasks are created.

Navigate to the transaction code /SCWM/MON and then go to Documents. Warehouse tasks

Five warehouse tasks are Confirmed in the Background.

 

Conclusion:

The Process flow steps for Single Order Staging is provided.

entry.title: How to change the destination stock type of a HU - Usage of BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT
entry.content:
Requirement:During internal transfer posting of a Full HU (/SCWM/ADHU) or a Product transfer( /SCWM/ADPROD) using Fiori or RF transaction, there shall be a capability of switching the destination stock type from its source one. If source HU has stock type has F2, the destination stock type of HU should be Q4 or B6. This is primarily used in industry where HU or partial HU stock will undergo Tempering or Thawing process( A kind of Quarantine for specific Hour),a process of improving the state or characteristics  of the product. After certain hours when tempering time is over, the Stock type should be automatically changed from QQ to FF in the same storage type via background job reading HU custom field.

Problem Addressed/ Standard Scenario: There is no standard functionality available in SAP which allows to flip the destination HU stock type on the fly when it is internally moved from one storage type A to destination Storage type B. One way is to hard code the 'Non Location Independent Stock Type'' QQ or BB in the destination storage type. However, the major pitfall is the storage type will never allow the posting change from QQ or BB to FF in Standard.

Solution: This blog will showcase a solution how to flip the stock type even hard coding the the 'Non Location Independent Stock Type'' QQ or BB to F2 in config .Also, there is no Badi available in EWM landscape which allows to change destination stock type at Warehouse task level. In the past there was BADI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP which allowed to change everything on WT level, this door is now closed unfortunately. 

The above issue is raised in customer connection as well.

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

However, there is workaround of flipping the stock type using Badi /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT.  This BAdI Allows  to determine the destination stock type in the case of automatic posting change upon warehouse task confirmation.

The current logic of stock type change is based on certain condition when a HU is internally moved from Storage type A to Storage Type B( QQ Hard code), system will flip the stock type from FF to QQ which is all standard. However after quarantine hour is over, the posting change from Monitor, /SCWM/POST, using  program /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE or using FM /SCWM/STOCK_CHANGE  allows to change the stock type from QQ to FF using BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT. 

In Standard, once storage type stock category is hard coded, it never allows to switch from QQ to FF or BB. System will always give an error message stock type change is not allowed. Hence, the above BAdI helps to ve multiple stock type even storage type allows single Stock category.

Storage type Config: The storage type has stock category as 'QQ'. Here QQ stock category is without QM. Customer can also chose BB if HU needs some rest time before it is switched back to FF.

In nutshell, the solution revolves into 2 aspects.

  1. Movement of HU from X to Y storage type and posting change from FF to QQ via Storage type config
  2. Posting change of HU from QQ to FF, the destination stock type change upon WT confirmation along with BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT to skip hard coded configuration.

  1. Perform a internal Transfer of an HU

The stock type changes to Q4 based on standard configuration.

Perform the stock type change from Q4 to F2 via Warehouse Monitor considering tempering time is over.

 

Post BAdI implementation, system would allow to flip the stock type disregarding storage type configuration.

Badi details:

To allow the scope of this BAdI only for tempering storage type, we have added an external switch so to influence the stock type change specific for tempering storage type . For other storage type, the logic will not influence and execute as per standard logic.

Below is the switch created which is public attribute and has been utilized externally to set 'X'.

Thanks for reading.

The blog has been co-authored by @prem_swaroop from SAP.

entry.title: Supply Chain in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
This blog post provides an overview of innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release 2408 for the line of business Supply Chain. 

Table of Contents

Order Promising

  • Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP (‌‌‌‌▶️demo video)
  • CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution

Inventory Management

  • Manage Physical Inventory Count app: Download–Upload Option (‌‌▶️demo video)
  • Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences (‌‌▶️demo video)
  • Special Stock E & K,  restricted-use stocks in Transfer Stock Apps (‌‌▶️demo video) 

Warehousing Management

  • Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order (▶️demo video)
  • Generation of Packaging Items (▶️demo video)
  • Partial Goods Receipt Posting (▶️demo video)

Transportation Management

  • Supplier Return with Transportation Management  (▶️demo video) + ...

More Information / Additional links

First watch the video from my teammate Markus Oertelt in which he presents his release highlights for supply chain management. 

Video 1: Watch the highlights by Markus Oertelt

 

Order Promising

Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP

Third-Party Order Processing (TPOP) with advanced ATP (aATP) now enables the processing of order items that are shipped to a subsidiary or plant. You can also use a new Business Add-In (BAdI) to modify the substitution result from Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC) to, for example, exclude substitutions from being used for confirmations and influence their priority.

In addition to the existing shipment type Third-Party Direct Shipment, you can now define a sourcing profile for third-party substitution strategies with the new shipment type Bought-In Item. This means the third-party order items shall be shipped to an own plant or subsidiary (instead of to the customer directly). In the sales order, the corresponding subitems get item category CBAB and also lead to a full confirmation in the availability check.

Using the new BAdI, Enriched Substitution Result in aATP, you can adapt the substitution result found by ABC. You can remove found substitutes from the list (for example, because their source of supply shall not be used for confirmations) and influence the priority of substitutions used for confirmations.

For the above, the Configure Substitution Strategy app has been enhanced with Advanced Configuration options (Sourcing Profile, Follow-Up Strategies, and Business Add-In) at substitution strategy level. Furthermore, a new subscreen (Follow-Up Strategies) for the configuration of follow-up strategies has been added. You can use follow-up strategies to enable additional confirmations to be used to fulfill outstanding requested quantity for requirements. After the availability of a requirement has been checked, the follow-up strategy is applied to any remaining quantity that could not be confirmed by the substitution strategy (or the preceding follow-up strategy if multiple follow-up strategies have been assigned to the substitution strategy.) If multiple follow-up strategies are defined, they are applied in the sequence defined by the ordinal number in the Configure Substitution Strategy app.

Discover the intricacies of third-party order processing through three detailed cases that Andreas Krause showcase in the video below: delegation to supplier for fulfillment, dynamic fulfillment decision (starting at 2:21 min), and a scenario in which we do not want that our supplier send the goods directly to our customer (starting at 6:00 min).

Video 2: Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution

The latest 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition brings an exciting innovation in the area of extensibility. We released the new CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution (CDS View Name I_SDScheduleLineWthOpenRqmtQty) which allows you to read for single open requirements in sales and distribution. From a technical standpoint, the data is read from table VBBE, which contains these entries.
This new CDS view opens up a world of possibilities for businesses looking to integrate their planning solutions with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. By consuming the data provided by the CDS view, companies can now seamlessly integrate their planning processes with their SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment.

Figure 1: CDS view 'Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution'

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Inventory Management

Manage Physical Inventory Count app: Download–Upload Option

The Manage Physical Inventory Count app has been enhanced. A new feature to download and upload Excel files for stock counting allow customers with 3rd-party warehouses to provide printouts to their staff. Once this kind of paper-based physical inventory process is completed, the results can be uploaded back into the system as an Excel file. Watch the next video in which I show you the new Excel download/upload option in Manage Physical Inventory Count app.

Video 3: Manage Physical Inventory Count app - download and upload Excel files

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences 

Now you can maintain tolerance groups for the posting of physical inventory differences to define the maximum value difference a user is allowed to post in physical inventory. For this configuration step, you define tolerance groups and assign user to the tolerance groups.

Figure 2: Configuration Step 'Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences''

Watch the video below in which I show you the configuration step and its impact on the physical inventory count.

Video 4: Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Special Stock E & K,  restricted-use stocks in SAP FIORi apps for performing stock changes 

You can use different stock types and special stock types to perform stock changes. The following table gives you an overview about the stock types and special stock types that are supported by the SAP Fiori apps Manage Stock, Transfer Stock - In-Plant, and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant. Blue highlighted stock types are newly supported since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. In detail: 

  • The special stock types Order Stock (E) and Consignment Stock (K) have been added to the Manage Stock and the Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.
  • You can now also display the stock type Restricted-Use Stock in the Transfer Stock - In-Plant and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.

Figure 3: Supported Stock Types and Special Stock Types in SAP Fiori apps for Goods Movement

In the next video I show you the use of the new stock types and special stock types in the SAP Fiori apps for performing stock changes.

Video 5: Use of the stock types and special stock types that are newly supported in the SAP Fiori apps 'Manage Stock', 'Transfer Stock - In-Plant', and 'Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant'

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Warehousing Management

Check out the blog post Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release – What’s New in which Christian Ries shares the innovations for Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release.

In this blog post I highlight 3 innovations from Warehouse Management that my colleague Markus Oertelt demonstrated in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series, and share the demo videos with you.

Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

Now you can split packed and unpacked items, quantities, or handling units (HUs) from an existing outbound delivery order into a new outbound delivery order.
Use this feature to remove parts of an outbound delivery order that, for example, are not ready to be shipped or for which there is no space on the transport, by splitting those parts into a new outbound delivery order.
You can split the outbound delivery order either before or after warehouse processing has started with created or confirmed warehouse tasks. Access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available in the Warehouse Monitor for the following nodes:

  • Outbound Delivery Order --> Outbound Delivery Order Item --> More Methods --> Split Open Qty into new ODO
  • Documents --> Handling Unit --> More methods --> Split into new ODO

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the warehouse-driven split of the outbound delivery order through two different use cases in the system:

  1. Use case - Complete picking is not possible
  2. Use case - Lack of space in the truck

Video 6: Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Generation of Packaging Items

For packed outbound deliveries packing material items can be generated automatically. The packing materials are posted goods issue in Warehouse Management (WM) and in Inventory Management (MM-IM). Generated items can be billing relevant. Items are generated with goods issue or with ready for shipping.

In the next video Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system and how to set it up in configuration. 

Video 7: Generation of Packaging Items

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Partial Goods Receipt Posting

Now, you can post goods receipt for one or several handling units (HUs) that belong to the same delivery. You can post goods receipt for parts of the inbound delivery that are packed into HUs (Partial Goods Receipt). 
You can access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available on the level of the Handling Unit node:

  • Goods Receipt
    This function posts goods receipt for one or more HUs.
  • Reverse Goods Receipt
    This function reverses goods receipt for one or more HUs.

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system.

Video 8: Partial Goods Receipt Posting

This feature is also available when you use radio frequency (RF) devices to carry out warehouse activities.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Transportation Management

Exciting product updates coming your way for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. I want to encourage you to read the latest blog post from Markus Oertelt 

  • Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 

that includes an amazing system demo about Supplier Return with Transportation Management and many more interesting topics.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

More Information / Additional links

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts from Markus Oertelt and me here:

  •  SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and @GerhardWelker.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

 

️Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

(🔙 Table of Contents)

entry.title: SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 - What's new?
entry.content:
SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 is now available on the App Store. Let's have a look at the new features. 

With SAP Warehouse Operator 2.4, we are introducing these new key capabilities: 

  • Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units
  • New Targeted Scan to better select a specific barcode among many barcodes
  • Support for low stock checks when confirming warehouse tasks
  • Basic validation profiles to configure required validation fields based on warehouse number and process type
  • Picking of partial quantities from Handling Units

Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units

The creation of Ad-Hoc warehouse tasks for Handling Units is now supported. If you want to move a Handling Unit inside a warehouse, but no warehouse task has been created yet, you may now use the app to create the warehouse task directly on your iPhone. Simply by scanning the Handling Unit and defining the warehouse process type for the task.

You can then walk or drive to the destination bin in the warehouse and scan it with the mobile app to finalize the creation of the task. Of course, you may also directly drop-off the Handling Unit and confirm the task.

 

Targeted Scan

There are occasions in a warehouse, when you want to select a specific barcode among many barcodes. For example, when changing the destination bin or the resource. To better guide the user for these cases, we are introducing a Targeted Scan. A finder on the screen supports the user to better aim at specific barcodes. By tapping on a highlighted barcode on the screen, it is then scanned and selected.

 

Low Stock Checks

The low stock check is a physical inventory procedure that you perform when confirming warehouse taks. Here you assume that a storage bin should only contain a small quantity of a product after a stock removal in accordance with the storage bin data in EWM. You define this quantity in the form of a limit value that is based on the operative unit of measure for the product per storage type. 

The system automatically activates the low stock check as soon as you create a warehouse task for a storage bin that should lie below the defined limit value after the stock removal. After conforming the warehouse task in SAP Warehouse Operator, the mobile app asks you to enter the remaining stock quantity in the storage bin.

 

Validation Profiles

Sometimes, it’s not needed to scan and validate all data when working on a warehouse task. Maybe the product IDs have not been printed out as barcodes in your warehouse. With the help of simple configuration profiles, it’s now possible to define which data doesn’t have to be scanned and validated using the mobile app.   

An administrator may configure the validation file and store it in Mobile Services. The file then gets fetched during the onboarding of the app and the rules are applied to the handling of the warehouse tasks. The validation profiles allow you to skip validation for product IDs and batches specific to a process type and warehouse.  

 

Additional Features

We have also enabled picking from Handling Units. However, only the picking of partial quantities is supported. Full palette picking is not supported.  

SAP Warehouse Operator now also supports Branding and Theming. So, the app can be made available to employees of your company following a consistent user experience.

Do you want to give us feedback inside the mobile app? Please tap on the new Feedback button in the user profile, which will lead you to survey to provide feedback.

 

Try the app and engage with us

Please download the new version of the app and try it out yourself.

 

 

Please leave a comment below if you have questions or feedback and we’ll get back to you.

You may also contact us at SAP_Warehouse_Operator@sap.com if you have questions on the configuration to use the app, the setup process or have specific requirements.

We also encourage you to visit our SAP Mobile Applications community page and click “follow”. We’ll be publishing more informative blog posts. Please post questions in the SAP Extended Warehouse Management area. Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Controlling internal movement of an expired Batch and blocked stock type- BAdI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP
entry.content:
Requirement: During internal transfer posting of a Full HU (/SCWM/ADHU) or a Product transfer( /SCWM/ADPROD) using Fiori or RF transaction, there shall be a capability of stopping HU/Product movement based on certain condition.

 Business Conditions:

  • Don't allow internal movement of HU/Product if Batch is expired
  • Don't allow internal movement of HU/Product if stock type is Blocked.

Problem Addressed/ Standard Scenario: There is no standard functionality available in SAP which allows to control the movement of HU/Product based on above condition.

Solution: SAP EWM offers a BAdI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP which can be used to put a validation on lot of business conditions. This blog will showcase a solution how to this BADI. There could be many usage of this BAdI, and primarily this is used for changing the consolidation group of WT during WO creation.( This BAdI gets triggered every time when each WT is assigned to WO).However, this is extremely critical BAdI in EWM and its usage is at lot of places before WO generation. In this blog, the business conditions of stopping internal movement of Expired & Blocked stock would be controlled using the BAdI  /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP.

Replication Steps: During Bin to Bin transfer from Component warehouse to Production supply( Adhoc), system should forbid the movement of HU reading the condition from BAdI.

Using RF: Create & Conf ADHOC HU

Using /SCWM/ADHU:

 

Using RF: Create & Conf ADHOC Product

Using /SCWM/ADPROD:

BAdI details: The BAdI uses interface /SCWM/IF_EX_WHO_DSTGRP using importing parameter IT_TO which allows to read HU WT( FLGHUTO), Warehouse Process Type( PROCTY=3), Stock Type( CAT=BB), Batch( CHARG), Shelf Life Expiration Date( VFDAT< current date), Product(MATID) & Warehouse Task(TANUM). Reading the above attribute before WT creation( HU or Product), a validation is put inside the BAdI.

Other BAdIs: Customer can also use /SCWM/EX_CORE_CR_INT_CR. This BAdI is specifically designed for Internal Process and customer can put validation. This BAdI is called during every WT creation.
In this BAdI, you can read the Batch ID, HU,VFDAT, Stock type and abort WT creation using parameter setting to ET_BAPIRET. This would work both HU WT and Adhoc Product WT.

Thanks for reading. All test is performed in S/4HANA 2022 Embedded EWM.

The blog is co-authored by @prem_swaroop

entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Choosing the Ideal SAP Warehousing Solution for Your Company
entry.content:
In today's dynamic supply chain landscape, businesses require efficient and flexible warehouse management systems (WMS) to stay competitive. SAP offers robust solutions for optimizing warehouse operations. However, with multiple options available, such as Basic EWM, Advanced EWM, Decentralized EWM, and newer alternatives like SAP Stock Room Management and SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud, choosing the right one can be challenging.

This blog will explore each of these options and provide guidance on selecting the most suitable solution for your business.

Understanding the Different SAP Warehouse Management Options

1. SAP Stock Room Management

  • What It Is: Stock Room Management is a simplified warehouse management solution introduced within SAP S/4HANA. It offers core warehouse functionalities tailored for businesses with straightforward warehouse needs. SAP created Stock Room Management for customers migrating from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system to S/4HANA, but who do not require the advanced features of SAP EWM.
  • Key Features:
    • Core warehouse processes like stock transfers, goods receipt, and goods issue.
    • Basic inventory management functionalities.
    • Material handling, cycle counting, and internal movements.
    • Simplified user experience with integration into SAP S/4HANA.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses that require basic warehouse functionalities without the need for advanced automation or complex workflows. It is ideal for smaller warehouses or companies transitioning from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management system who want to stay within the SAP S/4HANA ecosystem without upgrading to full EWM.

2. SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud

  • What It Is: SAP Warehouse Management (WM) on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-based warehouse management functionalities for businesses that want to move their operations to the cloud. It offers core WM features while leveraging the scalability and flexibility of the public cloud infrastructure.
  • Key Features:
    • Cloud-based deployment with access to core warehouse functionalities.
    • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • Real-time inventory visibility and warehouse operations.
    • Regular updates from SAP without the need for heavy customizations.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses looking for a scalable, cloud-based solution to manage basic warehouse operations, especially those that want to reduce infrastructure costs and simplify system management. It is an excellent option for companies transitioning to the cloud or that want a more agile and scalable warehouse management solution.

3. Basic EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Basic EWM is the foundational version of SAP EWM, embedded within SAP S/4HANA. It provides essential warehouse management features with no additional licensing costs.
  • Key Features:
    • Basic warehouse processes such as picking, packing, and goods receipt/goods issue.
    • Inventory management and stock transfers.
    • Integration with SAP S/4HANA for seamless data flow.
  • Best Suited For: Small to medium-sized businesses or warehouses with simple processes that don’t require advanced features like automation or complex workflows.

4. Advanced EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Advanced EWM is the more feature-rich version of SAP EWM, offering a comprehensive set of advanced warehouse management functionalities within SAP S/4HANA.
  • Key Features:
    • Complex warehouse operations, including cross-docking, wave management, slotting, and labor management.
    • Full support for automation systems (AGVs, conveyors).
    • Advanced stock management, including batch management and serialization.
    • Yard management, kitting, and value-added services.
  • Best Suited For: Medium to large businesses with complex warehouse processes that require high levels of automation and optimization.

5. Decentralized EWM

  • What It Is: Decentralized EWM is a stand-alone version of SAP EWM that operates on a separate server from the SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system, offering greater flexibility and scalability.
  • Key Features:
    • Runs independently from the SAP ERP system for improved flexibility.
    • Comprehensive functionality similar to Advanced EWM.
    • Designed for large warehouses with high transaction volumes and complex operations.
    • Multi-warehouse support and real-time process control.
  • Best Suited For: Large, global enterprises with multiple warehouses, complex operations, and high transaction volumes, where independence from the ERP system is crucial for uninterrupted operations.

Key Factors to Consider When Choosing the Right SAP Warehouse Management Solution

1. Warehouse Complexity

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are designed for businesses with straightforward warehouse operations. They are ideal if your processes are mostly manual, and you don’t require complex workflows or advanced automation.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer more comprehensive features, such as integration with automation, real-time process monitoring, and optimization. These are best for businesses with intricate warehouse setups, multiple locations, or advanced inventory management needs.
  • SAP WM on S/4HANA Public Cloud is suitable for companies seeking a cloud-first approach, providing core functionalities but with scalability and real-time access to inventory data.

2. Automation Needs

  • If your warehouse relies on automation or is planning to integrate automated systems (e.g., conveyors, AGVs, robots), you will need the capabilities of Advanced EWM or Decentralized EWM. These solutions offer robust support for integrating automation with your warehouse processes.
  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are better suited for manual operations or warehouses with low levels of automation.

3. Cloud Adoption Strategy

  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud is the optimal choice if your business is looking to adopt a cloud-based WMS. It provides flexibility, regular updates, and reduced infrastructure costs, making it ideal for companies focused on digital transformation and scalability.
  • For businesses that prefer an on-premise system or already have SAP S/4HANA in place, Embedded EWM (Basic or Advanced) or Decentralized EWM are viable options.

4. Cost and Licensing

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are included with SAP S/4HANA, making them cost-effective solutions for businesses that don’t need the additional features provided by Advanced or Decentralized EWM.
  • Advanced EWM requires additional licensing, but it offers significantly more functionality for businesses with complex warehouse needs.
  • Decentralized EWM comes with higher costs, as it requires separate infrastructure and licensing, but it is essential for large enterprises that need a fully independent warehouse management system.

5. Future Growth and Scalability

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are suitable for businesses with relatively stable warehouse operations that don’t anticipate significant changes in the near future.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer scalability, making them ideal for growing businesses or enterprises with plans to expand their warehouse operations, implement automation, or add new distribution centers.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-native scalability, making it an excellent choice for companies looking to grow their operations quickly and cost-effectively while leveraging cloud infrastructure.

Conclusion: Which SAP Warehouse Management Solution is Right for You?

Choosing the right SAP warehouse management solution depends on your business’s operational complexity, growth plans, and technological direction. Here's a summary of when each solution may be the best fit:

  • Stock Room Management: Best for businesses with simple warehouse operations that are moving from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system and want to continue with basic functionality within SAP S/4HANA.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud: Ideal for companies focused on adopting cloud solutions with core warehouse functionality and scalability.
  • Basic EWM: Perfect for small to medium-sized businesses that need essential warehouse management features as part of their SAP S/4HANA system without additional costs.
  • Advanced EWM: Suited for medium to large businesses with more complex warehouse operations, requiring advanced automation, inventory management, and process optimization.
  • Decentralized EWM: The best choice for large, multi-warehouse businesses that need a fully independent WMS, capable of handling high volumes and offering real-time, 24/7 operations.

By assessing your current and future warehouse needs, transaction volumes, levels of automation, and growth strategy, you can make an informed decision and choose the right SAP warehouse management solution to drive operational efficiency and support your business objectives.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE - DSC Knowledge Bits - Cookbook for Advanced Shipping and Receiving
entry.content:
Dear TM and EWM friends and fans,

hopefully all of you have heard about Advanced Shipping and Receiving (Adv. SR, sometimes referred to as ASR) by now, which is the new integration between S/4 HANA TM and S/4 HANA EWM. If not, I'd recommend this blog article by @Jörg_Michaelis  for an introduction and this blog article for some more insights. Additionally check out this Learning Journey and then you should have a good understanding of the topic 😉

Now, SAP have released a new Consulting Note 3512117 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving - Cookbook which includes a holistic set-up and customizing guide as well as a ste-by-step guide how to execute best practise scenarios. Hopefully this will help customers, partners and consultants with implementing best practice scenarios. Please note, this is a first version based on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 and going forwards the cookbook will be updated and maintained to stay on track with latest changes and improvements.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – New Migration Guide LE-WM to EWM on S/4HANA
entry.content:
In this blog, I would like to share the latest updates on the new migration guide that was released for supporting customers and consultants during planning the migration from SAP ERP LE-WM or SAP S/4HANA Stock Room Management to EWM on S/4HANA. 

Additionally, I would like to clear up some confusion about end dates of usage rights and mainstream maintenance for SAP LE-WM.

LE-WM Roadmap - Important Dates:
Let's start with the SAP LE-WM roadmap and timeline, as this is mainly dependent on the ERP solution release on which LE-WM is running.

  • LE-WM on ERP ECC:
    SAP will provide mainstream maintenance until 31.12.2027 for SAP Business Suite 7 core applications, refer to SAP Note 2881788 for details.

  • LE-WM on ERP S/4HANA:
    SAP restricts the usage of components which are in a compatibility mode until 31.12.2025, refer to SAP Notes 2269324, 2881166, and 2577428 for details.

Migration Guide Overview:
In this migration guide you will find information about:

  • General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM
  • System Setup Guides
  • Organizational Structure
  • Customizing Settings for Product Master
  • Define Handling Unit Management
  • Storage Bin Migration
  • Putaway and Stock Removal
  • Material Master Migration
  • Transactional Data Migration
  • Stock Migration
  • Physical Inventory Completeness Migration

Out of Scope:

  • In general, transactional data is not transferred from LE-WM to EWM. So no documents can be open when the migration starts. For more information, see Transactional Data Migration.
  • Existing RF transactions are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • System users and the related authorization roles and profiles are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • The existing migration tools do not migrate Customizing. Due to the broader functionality in EWM, the Customizing differs from LE-WM. For more information, see General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM.
  • Customer-specific objects are not transferred

SAP Note link where you can download the mgiration guide: https://me.sap.com/notes/3523591.

Thanks to the EWM product management and CoE teams for the preparation of this useful document.

Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer and you are interested in knowing more about SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Extended Warehouse Management solution, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to arrange for Architecture Point of View (APoV) service.

#CoE EMEA SCM1

entry.title: Create Inbound Deliveries in EWM Using an ABAP Program (Save Inbound Delivery Replica)
entry.content:
Recently, I encountered a requirement where Purchase Order data was being sourced from a non-SAP ERP system. This data was stored in our custom Z tables within the EWM system. The goal was to enable warehouse users to create inbound delivery replicas using RF devices or a front-end transaction in a decentralized EWM environment.

As a novice in EWM system development, I was unsure about the most efficient way to accomplish this task. Therefore, I will begin by outlining my approach and the solution I discovered.

There are two standard ways I could create Inbound Delivery in EWM:

  1. Transaction: /SCWM/PRDI
  2. Standard Inbound IDoc: SHP_IBDLV_SAVE_REPLICA

Creating the delivery using the transaction was not feasible, as it didn’t align with the user’s needs. An IDoc was the only option for automating the delivery creation process. However, using an IDoc would require either triggering it via a file or establishing outbound and inbound partner profiles for my system, followed by externally triggering the outbound IDoc from my ABAP program.

  1. Debugging /SCWM/PRDI transaction: Debugging this transaction felt too complex and time-consuming, so I skipped this and went ahead with debugging IDoc function module 
  2. Testing and debugging IDoc (SHP_IBDLV_SAVE_REPLICA): The delivery was successfully created using the WE19 IDoc test transaction. I further proceeded to debug the IDoc function module. During this process, I identified a class that was utilized for creating inbound
    • Class: /scwm/cl_mapin_id_savereplica

While analyzing the function group for the IDoc function module, I discovered another function module within the same group.

/SCWM/INB_DLV_SAVEREPLICA: This function module, also creates EWM Inbound Deliveries using the same class and methods as the IDoc. Therefore, I decided to investigate whether I could utilize this function module in my ABAP program to create inbound deliveries.
