As an additional note, this function module is also used to replicate Inbound Deliveries from SAP ERP systems using RFCs.

After a few trials and resolving quite a few errors, I discovered that this function module required qRFC to successfully create inbound deliveries. Invoking it without qRFC would result in an ABAP runtime error or dump.

Following this, I have included an example program that demonstrates the use of the function module for creating inbound deliveries.

 

Create EWM inbound delivery replica using FM: /SCWM/INB_DLV_SAVEREPLICA

The following demo program illustrates how to create outbound and inbound queues using ABAP qRFC APIs and invoke the function as an RFC in a separate task.

Note: You must have an SM59 ABAP destination configured for your EWM system.

Program:

 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZTEST_INBOUND_DELIVERY
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ZTEST_INBOUND_DELIVERY.

DATA: lt_header_partner          TYPE /scwm/tt_header_partner,
      lt_header_deadlines        TYPE /scwm/tt_header_deadlines,
      lt_item_data               TYPE /scwm/tt_inb_item_data,
      lt_item_org                TYPE /scwm/tt_inb_item_org,
      lt_item_ref_purchase_order TYPE /scwm/tt_item_ref_prchse_order.


*Creating work areas.
DATA: ls_header_data   TYPE /scwm/bapiibdlvhdr,
      ls_header_org    TYPE /scwm/bapiibdlvhdrorg,
      lv_sender_system TYPE tbdls-logsys,
      ls_techn_control TYPE /scwm/bapidlvcontrol.

DATA: lv_matnr TYPE matnr VALUE '1'.



DATA: lv_idoc_status TYPE bdidocstat-status,
      lv_deliv_numb  TYPE vbeln_vl VALUE '4630900042'.


"Set Data into Import Parameters
ls_header_data = VALUE #( deliv_numb   = lv_deliv_numb
                          billoflading = 'TESTBOL'
                          extdelv_no   = |DEL_{ sy-datum }_{ sy-timlo }UTC|
                          dlv_type     = 'EL'
                          sd_doc_cat   = '7' ).

ls_header_org = VALUE #( ship_point = '0001'
                         whse_no    = 'W01' ).

lv_sender_system = 'NONSAP_ERP'.        "Define as Non-SAP ERP in SPRO configs

ls_techn_control = VALUE #( upd_ind        = 'I'
                            recv_whs_no    = 'W01'
                            recv_sys       = 'EWDCLNT100'
                            dlv_type       = 'EL'
                            internal_queue = abap_true ).

lt_header_partner = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb = lv_deliv_numb
                               itm_number = '000010'
                               partner_no = 'WH01VEN' ) ).

lt_header_deadlines = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb    = lv_deliv_numb
                                 timetype      = 'WSHDRLFDAT'
                                 timestamp_utc = '20240920150000'
                                 timezone      = 'UTC' ) ).

lt_item_data = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb        = lv_deliv_numb
                          itm_number        = '000010'
                          material          = |{ lv_matnr ALPHA = IN }|
                          dlv_qty           = '5.000'
                          sales_unit_iso    = 'EA'
                          sales_unit        = 'EA'
                          dlv_qty_stock     = '5.000'
                          base_uom          = 'EA'
                          base_uom_iso      = 'EA'
                          conv_unit_nom     = '1'
                          conv_unit_denom   = '1'
                          rec_point         = '0001'
                          item_categ        = 'ELN'
                          whse_mvmt         = '101'
                          move_type         = '101'
                          move_type_wm      = '101'
                          material_external = '1'
                          prod_date         = sy-datum ) ).

lt_item_org = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb = lv_deliv_numb
                         itm_number = '000010'
                         plant      = 'WH01'
                         stge_loc   = 'WH01' ) ).

lt_item_ref_purchase_order = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb     = lv_deliv_numb
                                        itm_number     = '000010'
                                        doc_number     = '4500000001'
                                        itm_number_ref = '00010'
                                        sd_doc_cat     = 'V'
                                        doc_cat        = 'F'
                                        doc_type       = 'NB'
                                        status         = '9'
                                        pur_group      = '001'
                                        doc_date       = '20240911'
                                        gr_ind         = 'X'
                                        gr_basediv     = 'X' ) ).

DATA: lv_deliv TYPE vbeln.

CONSTANTS: lc_queue_name      TYPE trfcqout-qname VALUE 'TEST_QUEUE_EWM_1',
           lc_queue_name_in   TYPE trfcqout-qname VALUE 'TEST_QUEUE_EWM_2',
           lc_ewd_destination TYPE rfcdes-rfcdest VALUE 'EWD'.


"Set Queue Name for the next qRFC-call
CALL FUNCTION 'TRFC_SET_QUEUE_NAME'
  EXPORTING
    qname              = lc_queue_name
  EXCEPTIONS
    invalid_queue_name = 1
    OTHERS             = 2.
IF sy-subrc = 0.

  CALL FUNCTION 'TRFC_SET_QIN_PROPERTIES'
    EXPORTING
      qin_name           = lc_queue_name_in
      qout_name          = lc_queue_name
    EXCEPTIONS
      invalid_queue_name = 1
      error_message      = 2
      OTHERS             = 3.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.

    CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/INB_DLV_SAVEREPLICA'
      IN BACKGROUND TASK
      AS SEPARATE UNIT
      DESTINATION lc_ewd_destination
      EXPORTING
        techn_control           = ls_techn_control
        header_data             = ls_header_data
        header_org              = ls_header_org
        sender_system           = lv_sender_system
      TABLES
        header_partner          = lt_header_partner
        header_deadlines        = lt_header_deadlines
        item_data               = lt_item_data
        item_org                = lt_item_org
        item_ref_purchase_order = lt_item_ref_purchase_order.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      ROLLBACK WORK.
    ELSE.
      COMMIT WORK.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

WRITE: |Execution complete for queue|.

"As the FM does not return Delivery Number,
" we can wait and fetch the delivery from table
"  using PO number as refernce
WAIT UP TO 5 SECONDS.
SELECT docno AS delivery
      FROM /scdl/db_proch_i AS a
      INNER JOIN /scdl/db_refdoc AS b
      ON a~docid = b~docid
      WHERE b~refdoccat = 'IDR'
        AND b~doccat = 'PDI'
        AND b~refbskey = 'NONSAP_ERP'
        AND b~refdocno = _deliv_numb
        INTO TABLE (lt_inb_deliv).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  WRITE: |Delivery created Successfully|.
  cl_demo_output=>display( lt_inb_deliv ).
ENDIF.

entry.title: Full and partial pallet putaway storage type determination based on quantity classification
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Requirement : Full pallet and Partial pallet determination based on quantity classification during Putaway Process with quantity determination using Packspec condition records and not based on unit of measurement attributes.

Solution Configuration:

Step1 : Define quantity classifications under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Cross Process Settings--> Warehouse Task-->Define Quantity Classifications

Here I have taken the pallets as 4 

 

Step2: Maintain the Storage Type search sequence for putaway with quantity classifications under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Goods Receipt Process-->Strategies-->Storage Type Search-->Definition of Groups-->Specify storage type search sequence for Putaway

Here I am taking full pallet storage type as RPA1 with assign quantity classifier as 4 ( pallets ) and Partial pallet Storage type as LOSE with no quantity classifiers.

 

Step3: Update the Access sequence for putaway under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Goods Receipt Process-->Strategies-->Storage Type Search-->Definition of Groups-->Optimize access strategy for Storage Type search:Putaway

 

Step4: Update the Warehouse task Determination procedure in the Warehouse number control field under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Master Data-->Define Warehouse number control

I am using standard procedure 0WHTA. Here if the custom fields are added, you can update the new custom procedure

 

Master Data

Packspec Condition Records: Create the packspec Condition records for Packing(0IBD) and pallet quantity determination (0WHT).

I have taken product 100000001223 and its full pallet quantity is 100 KG

Autopack Condition Record

 

 

Full pallet Quantity determination condition record

 

 

 

Here update the quantity classifier as 4(pallets) and its respective HUType

 

 

Partial pallet Quantity Determination Condition record

Here we need to update the new condition record with sequence 2 (for partial pallets) and sequence 1 (for full pallets)

 

The min and target quantity should be updated with 1 and 99( as 100 is my full pallet quantity)

 

 

 

 

Test case: I have taken the delivery with product 100000001223 of 240 KG. Here, there are two full pallets of 100 KG and one partial pallet of 40 KG.

 

 

Here you can find the below product warehouse tasks created for HU

 

Hope that this post is clear for performing the full pallet and partial pallet withdrawal using quantity classification via condition records instead of UOM attributes.

Thanks,

Sudarshan Sathyanarayanan.

entry.title: Maximizing ROI from Your SAP EWM Investment: Expert Strategies for Continuous Optimization
entry.content:
Logistics Directors,  IT Program managers,  wonder if they are getting the most out of your SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) investment. If you feel like your warehouse operations aren’t fully optimized, you’re not alone. IT and logistics leaders often find themselves wondering if they’re truly maximizing ROI from their SAP systems. As a veteran with over three decades of experience in SAP implementations, I've seen countless organizations struggle to maximize their Return on Investment (ROI) from this powerful tool. In this article, we'll explore common challenges and proven strategies to unlock the full potential of your SAP EWM investment.

The SAP EWM ROI Challenge

Many organizations face similar hurdles when it comes to optimizing their SAP EWM systems:

  1. Inefficient processes that fail to leverage EWM's advanced capabilities
  2. Low user adoption due to inadequate training and support
  3. Limited data visibility, hindering informed decision-making

These issues aren't just minor setbacks – they're ROI killers. They result in slower order fulfillment, wasted resources, and widespread frustration across teams.

Expert Strategies for SAP EWM Optimization

Drawing from my 30+ years of experience, here are four key strategies to boost your SAP EWM ROI:

1. Harness Advanced Features

Task & Resource Management, RF Framework, Queue Management,  Slotting, Dynamic routing with POSC ( Process Oriented Storage Control) and LOSC ( Layout Oriented Storage Control),  Clustered Putaway, Wave Management,  Fixed Bin replenishment, Full pallet picking, Warehouse Monitor aren't just buzzwords. When properly implemented, they're game-changers for efficiency.

2. Conduct Regular Process Reviews

Your warehouse operations are dynamic, and your processes should be too. Implement quarterly workflow assessments to identify and eliminate inefficiencies.  SAP is heavily investing in EWM to roll out  new functionality with every release.  It is important to keep track of what is new and  what is relevant for continuous innovation of your EWM system.  Check this information at  SAP S/4HANA What’s New Viewer. Link to the viewer on SAP website is - https://help.sap.com/whats-new/5fc51e30e2744f168642e26e0c1d9be1  Please see below screenshot to filter it to EWM specific innovations.

 

 This ongoing optimization ensures your EWM system evolves with your business needs.

3. Prioritize Continuous Training

A one-time training session during implementation isn't enough. Develop a continuous learning program to keep your team proficient with SAP EWM. This approach not only improves system adoption but also empowers employees to leverage advanced features effectively. Encourage your subject matter experts (SME) to identify areas where users are making more mistakes and  focus on building focused training sessions on  those topics. These could be  scheduled as lunch and learn sessions as well without too much of training overheads.

4. Integrate Predictive Analytics

Combine SAP EWM with S/4HANA to unlock the power of real-time insights and predictive modeling. Some good examples could be open purchase orders / inbound deliveries / outbound deliveries to identify workload on warehouse. This integration can transform your decision-making  and planning capabilities, leading to smarter resource allocation and improved operational efficiency.

Remember, in the fast-paced world of logistics and supply chain management, standing still is equivalent to moving backward. It's time to optimize your SAP EWM investment and drive real, measurable ROI.

Are you facing challenges with your SAP EWM implementation? Share your experiences in the comments below, and let's discuss how to overcome them!

Shivaji Patil

SAP Warehousing Platinum Advisor

Genius Business Solutions, Inc.

entry.title: Advance Production Integration – E2E Process covering GI of components & GR of Finished Goods
entry.content:
Advanced Production Integration in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) streamlines the connection between production and warehouse operations. This integration allows businesses to effectively manage the supply of materials to production and the receipt of semi-finished or finished products back into the warehouse. This integration helps optimize material flows, improve inventory accuracy, and enhance overall operational efficiency, allowing businesses to quickly respond to changing market demands.

Advance production Integration supports 4 types of staging method – we will be covering Single Order Staging method in this blog. This blog will be divided in 2 parts – Configuration and testing.

I am using single warehouse - single storage location (AFS) for production and warehousing space in this blog.

Part – 1 Configuration:

S4 Configuration –

1. Create Material - Finished Good & Components (T-code – MM01)

First step will be creation of materials – create finished goods and components and maintain Basic Data, Sales View, Purchasing, MRP all views, Work Scheduling views.

I have created FG-11 as Finished Product and SM-11 & SM-12 as Components.

2. Create BOM for Finished Product (T-code - CS01)

Enter Material, Plant & BOM usage details – BOM usage should be 1 for production purpose. (For kitting or other operations – it will be different).

Enter components and required quantity of components to prepare finished product. The item category is “L” for stock Item.

I have taken 2 for each component to prepare our final product.

3. Create PSA – Production Supply Area (T-code – PK05)

Enter Plant, PSA, Storage Location & Responsible person name.

4. Create Work Center for Finished Product (T-code – CR01)

Add Usage – 009 – All task list types

Also, add cost center details under costing tab.

5. Routing for Finished Product (T-code – CA01)

Create Routing for finished product – it should list all the production steps.

For testing, I have maintained only 1 – 0010 as complete production step.

6. Assign Production scheduling profile & Production version to Finished Product (T-code – MM02)

Tab – Work Scheduling

7. Control cycle for components (T-code – LKP1)

Need to create control cycle maintenance for components – this will determine source & destination storage location and staging Indicator.

EWM Configuration –

1. BC Sets Activation (T-code – SCPR20)

Need to activate below BC sets, it will add document and item type related basic settings to generate PMR.

2. ERP-EWM Interface

PATH – SPRO – IMG – SCM EWM – EWM – INTERFACE – ERP INTEGRATION – PRODUCTION – MAP ORDER TYPE TO DOC TYPE

3. WPT & WPT Determination (GI & GR)

Create 2 WPT - I have used standard WPT - 2100 (Stock Removal) & 1100 WPT (Put away) and then maintain WPT determination criteria.

PATH – SPRO – IMG – SCM EWM – EWM – CROSS PROCESS SETTINGS – WAREHOUSE TASK – DEFINE WPT

4. Storage Strategies (Removal & Placement)

I have created simple strategies by using WPT only.

5. Maintain Product Master Data (T-code - /SCWM/MAT1)

Maintain control indicators if used any during WPT determination or storage search sequence.

6. Maintain Stock for components

Maintain stock for components through IB Delivery or uploading stock (/SCWM/ISU).

7. Create/Replicate PSA (T-code - /SCWM/PSA_REPLICATE)

8. Create Storage type, Production work center and related AA.

PATH – SPRO – IMG – SCM EWM – EWM – MASTER DATA – DEFINE STORAGE TYPE

Create 1 storage type for GR – 9015 and 1 for Production Supply – PROD (With storage type role – K)

Create Work center – PRD1

9. Master Data (Bin Creation, T-code - /SCWM/LS01)

Create bins – 1 for GR and 1 for production supply and then sort them by using T-code /SCWM/SBST.

Assign work center bin to work center (T-code - /SCWM/TWORKST)

10. Assigning bin to PSA (T-code - /SCWM/PSASTAGE)

Enter staging method, staging bin, staging WPT, replenishment qty if want to trigger more qty for replenishment purpose.

Part – 2 Testing:

1. Create Production Order (T-code – CO01)

Enter Finished product, Production Plant and Order type details.

Enter total quantity and Scheduling type details then navigate to component tab to see component view.

Release order and then Execute staging by clicking on Functions – WM Material Staging -Simulate & Execute and then save the document.

2. Review Order (T-code - /SCWM/PMR)

Review order document type, item type, finished product and component quantity, WPT and so on.

3. Create WT to stage components (T-code - /SCWM/STAGE)

Select any selection criteria, I am choosing order number and execute the staging, I am getting warning message as we have selected replenishment qty 5 in /SCWM/PSASTAGE t-code.

4. Confirm staging task (RFUI or directly from MON) and it will move stage the qty to stage bin.

We can check through manufacture order number.

5. Now, consumption will take place, and it will auto GI (T-code - /SCWM/RFUI)

Enter 451 to navigate to manufacturing screen directly and enter order no.

Standard screen will ask for below details – enter HU/Bin, product and quantity and hit enter to save the record.

Do this for both products, we can check the status in monitor w.r.t. manufacture order.

Verify the movement type 261 under manufacture order (T-code – CO03)

6. GR for finished product. We can use RF to create IB Del against the manufacture order.

Enter 3464 to navigate directly to receive screen by order number, system will ask work center – enter work center details and then enter the manufacture order no.

Enter the finished product details.

Next screen will ask for HU details – create HU by entering packaging material details (F2 ChPm) and update quantity (F3 ChQty) and then create HU (F4 HUCr). It will create IBD.

Review Inbound Delivery (T-code - /SCWM/PRDI), Pack Status shows completed, do the GR.

Review GR and movement types 101 back in the manufacture order (T-code – CO03)

Further, Put away can be executed in EWM for the finished product.

This is the simplest scenario in advance production integration and steps that can be followed to understand the concept of PMR, Staging methods and consumption and receiving of finished goods.

Thank you for your time and going over my work.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC SAP TM Archiving TOR Object
entry.content:
This blog aims to the simple step by step guide to do Archiving in SAP Transportation Management. This Note 2062861 - SAP TM Archiving explain lot of common issues faced.

The per-requisite for a freight order(TOR) to do Archiving is set Freight Order/Freight Unit in completed status.

1. Run Tcode – AOBJ (Transaction code to maintain the Archiving Object)

Following information is maintained for TOR Objects Archiving Object

 

Object Name – SCMTMSTOR name of the archiving object.

Write Program – Name of the program for writing data to archived files.

Delete Program – Name of the program for deleting data from DB which has been archived.

Preprocessing Program – Name of program for preprocessing of the data like validation checks.

2. Customization for Freight Order Type

In the SPRO, Maintain Archiving Retention Period in Days. This field will query the documents after completion Status for Archiving. The Archiving Date will be set to number of days plus the date on which the document is set to completed.

3. Call Transaction SARA and enter the Archive Object Name ‘SCMTMSTOR’

4. Preprocessing of Archive Data

This step is used to filter the data for Archiving based on selection criteria.

  • Create a new Variant as shown below.

  • Go back to initial screen and click on Start button. This will schedule the archiving job for Preprocessing program. In this example – I am executing it only once by choosing the immediate option.

Select the Spool Parameters based on system settings.

  • Click on Job Button to check the result data

Click on ‘Write’ button.

The button is used to execute the Write program for Archiving Objects.

Maintain the Variant and Run all the Steps as the  Pre-processing option. The result is again a spool table.

Click on ‘Delete’ button. This button would execute the delete program to delete the achieved data from the Database Table.

You can select the Archived file and set the schedule date on which data should be deleted from file.

Click the Archive Selection button and Select the TOR objects you want to Archive.

Read the Archived Data.

How to View Archive Data

  1. Run Tcode - AS_AFB(Archive File Brower)
